error.signature: Excel executes formulas when opening CSV exported from web application, enabling code execution

verdict.summary: CSV cells starting with =, +, -, @ are interpreted as formulas by Excel/Google Sheets. Exported user data containing '=HYPERLINK(evil_url)' or '=CMD|...' executes code when opened. OWASP lists this as an injection vulnerability.

dead_ends.action: Sanitize CSV output by HTML-encoding special characters
dead_ends.why_fails: HTML encoding doesn't help - Excel doesn't interpret HTML entities. The formula characters =+- must be escaped differently for CSV.

dead_ends.action: Assume CSV is 'just text' and doesn't need sanitization
dead_ends.why_fails: Spreadsheet applications auto-execute formulas. A cell starting with = is executed, even if it came from user input.

workarounds.action: Prefix dangerous cells with a single quote or tab character
workarounds.how: if cell.startswith(('=', '+', '-', '@')): cell = "'" + cell  # single quote prevents formula execution

workarounds.action: Wrap all cell values in double quotes and prepend space to formula-like values
Serve CSV with Content-Type: text/csv and Content-Disposition: attachment to prevent browser rendering
